reasoning: As of early 2025, Amazon's market capitalization stands around $2.35 trillion, trailing significantly behind Apple (approaching $4 trillion), Nvidia (~$3.3+ trillion), and Microsoft (~$3.1 trillion). Closing a gap of over $1.5 trillion in less than 3 months (from Jan to late March) or even 15 days (from the mid-March forecast date) is practically impossible absent an unprecedented simultaneous collapse of its three larger rivals coupled with an apocalyptic surge for Amazon. The Polymarket pricing of 0.01 earlier in March reflects this near-zero chance. I have assigned the lowest allowable probability of 0.02.
